content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 21, 1991

Tony Sedano
General Manager
South San Bernardino Water District
882 S. Tippecanoe Avenue
San Bernardino, CA 92408

Re: Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-91-155

Dear Mr. Sedano:

This letter is in response to your request for confirmation
of the telephone advice provided to you on March 5, 1991,
regarding the duties and responsibilities of an employee of the
City of San Bernardino who is also a member of the Board of
Directors of the South San Bernardino Water District (the
district). In your capacity as general manager for the district
you requested our advice to determine whether under the conflict-
of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"),¹
the board member was disqualified from participating in decisions
regarding the merger of the district with the City of San
Bernardino.

You have not provided us with the name of the board member on
whose behalf you are seeking our advice. Accordingly, we treat
your letter as a request for informal assistance pursuant to the
provisions of Regulation 18329 (copy enclosed).²

_____________________________

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. I-91-155
Page No. 2

Your letter accurately reflects the advice provided to you.
Under the provisions of the Act, an employee of the City of San
Bernardino who is also a member of the board of directors of the
South San Bernardino Water District may vote on the proposed
merger of the South San Bernardino Water District with the City of
San Bernardino. This is so because for purposes of the Act,
salary from a public entity is not a disqualifying source of
income to a public official. (Section 82030(b).)

Please note that our advice is limited to the provisions of
the Act. There are other provisions of law which may affect the
resolution of the questions posed in your request for advice. We
suggest that you consult with the Office of the Attorney General
regarding other provisions of law such as the doctrine of
incompatible public office which may be applicable to your facts.

We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do
not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[signature]

By: Blanca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division
